title: Postal workers, mail sorters and messengers
description: Operate crematory equipment to reduce human or animal remains to bone fragments in accordance with state and local regulations. Duties may include preparing the body for cremation and performing general maintenance on crematory equipment. May use traditional flame-based cremation, calcination, or alkaline hydrolysis. Handle baggage for travelers at transportation terminals or for guests at hotels or similar establishments. All personal care and service workers not listed separately. (Based on 23 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Sort and route incoming and outgoing mail, according to type and destination, by hand or using electronic mail-sorting and scanning devices; clear jams in sorting equipment; accept and check containers of mail or parcels from large volume mailers, couriers, and contractors.

Operate various types of equipment, such as computer scanning equipment, addressographs, mimeographs, optical character readers, and bar-code sorters; scan labels on letters or parcels to confirm receipt; check items to ensure that addresses are legible and correct, that sufficient postage has been paid, and that items are in suitable condition for processing.

Deliver mail to residences and business establishments along specified routes by walking or driving, using a combination of satchels, carts, cars, and small trucks; return to the post office with mail collected from homes, businesses, and public mailboxes; bundle mail in preparation for delivery or transportation to relay boxes.

Load and unload mail trucks and manually sort odd-sized mail by hand, segregating items requiring special handling; distribute incoming mail into the correct boxes or pigeonholes; operate machines that automatically feed mail and observe operations to detect malfunctions.

Affix postage to packages or letters by hand or using postage meters and verify that items are addressed correctly, marked with the proper postage, and in suitable condition for processing; answer customers' questions about postal services and regulations; respond to complaints regarding mail theft, delivery problems, and lost or damaged mail.

Provide customers with change of address cards and other forms; maintain accurate records of deliveries; collect signed receipts for registered, certified, and insured mail and complete necessary paperwork; remove incorrectly addressed mail to senders and hold mail for customers who are away from delivery locations.

Weigh letters and parcels to determine the postage needed using weighing scales and rate charts and prepare mail for delivery to mailing facilities; answer inquiries regarding shipping or mailing policies; and inspect mail machine output for defects and determine how to eliminate any causes of defects.

Train new workers; check mail to ensure correct postage and that packages and letters are in proper condition for mailing; complete forms that notify publishers of address changes and perform errands for customers or employers, such as delivering or picking up mail and packages.
Move containers of mail using equipment such as forklifts and automated 'trains' and load and unload trucks or vehicles, ensuring the transportation of materials to and from specified locations.

Seal or open envelopes by hand or using machines; wrap packages or bundles by hand or using tying machines; and remove mail from machines and feed mail into postage canceling devices or hand stamp mail to cancel postage.

Travel to post offices to pick up mail for routes or from postal relay boxes, and record address changes and redirect mail for those addresses, ensuring that all tasks comply with established procedures and deadlines.
Use and maintain tools or equipment found on commercial vehicles, and keep money drawers in order while recording and balancing daily transactions.

Clean the crematorium, including tables, floors, and equipment. Document divided remains to ensure parts are not misplaced. Embalm, dress, or otherwise prepare the deceased for viewing. Explain the cremation process to family or friends. Offer counsel and comfort to bereaved families or friends. Pick up and handle human or pet remains in a respectful manner. Place corpses into crematory machines to reduce remains to bone fragments using flame, heat, or alkaline hydrolysis. Pulverize remaining bone fragments into smaller pieces, using specialized equipment, such as a cremulator or grinder. Remove jewelry, watches, or other personal items from the deceased prior to cremation. Read documentation to confirm the identity of the deceased.

Transfer luggage, trunks, and packages to and from rooms, loading areas, vehicles, or transportation terminals, by hand or using baggage carts. Perform routine vehicle maintenance, such as regulating tire pressure and adding gasoline, oil, and water. Pick up or meet passengers according to requests, appointments, or schedules. Prepare and submit reports that may include the number of passengers or trips, hours worked, mileage driven fuel consumed, or fares received. Supply guests or travelers with directions, travel information, and other information, such as available services and points of interest. Receive and mark baggage by completing and attaching claim checks. Greet incoming guests and escort them to their rooms. Deliver messages and room service orders, and run errands for guests. Explain the operation of room features, such as locks, ventilation systems, and televisions. Maintain clean lobbies or entrance areas for travelers or guests. Transport guests about premises and local areas, or arrange for transportation. Pick up and return items for laundry and valet service. Check with home offices after completed deliveries to confirm deliveries and collections and to receive instructions for other deliveries.

Inspect or test materials, stock, vehicles, equipment, or facilities to ensure that they are safe, free of defects, and consistent with specifications. Monitor field work to ensure proper performance and use of materials. Enforce safety rules and regulations. Resolve worker problems or collaborate with employees to assist in problem resolution. Plan work assignments and equipment allocations to meet transportation, operations or production goals. Confer with customers, supervisors, contractors, or other personnel to exchange information or to resolve problems. Drive vehicles or operate machines or equipment to complete work assignments or to assist workers. Maintain or verify records of time, materials, expenditures, or crew activities. Distribute messages and items, such as newspapers, documents, and packages, to appropriate locations.

Remove jewelry, watches, or other personal items from the deceased prior to cremation. Prepare documents and correspondence, such as damage claims, credit and billing inquiries, invoices, and service complaints. Gather records pertinent to specific problems, review them for completeness and accuracy, and attach records to correspondence as necessary.

Observe and evaluate workers' appearance and performance to ensure quality service and compliance with specifications. Inspect work areas or operating equipment to ensure conformance to established standards in cleanliness and maintenance. Inspect materials, stock, vehicles, equipment, or facilities to ensure they are safe and free of defects.
Explain and demonstrate work tasks to new workers or assign training tasks to experienced workers. Train workers in proper operational procedures and functions and explain company policies.

Direct or coordinate the activities of workers and resolve customer complaints regarding performance or services rendered. Confer with customers, supervisors, contractors, or other personnel to exchange information or resolve problems.

Recruit and hire staff members. Requisition necessary supplies, equipment, or services. Apply customer feedback to service improvement efforts. Recommend and implement measures to improve worker motivation, work methods, or customer services.
Take disciplinary action to address performance problems. Enforce safety rules and regulations.
Analyze and record personnel or operational data and write related activity reports. Compute or estimate cash, payroll, transportation, or personnel requirements.
